taxonomy_v3: Engineering and Development, Software Engineering, Backend Software Engineer
core_skills: C#, TypeScript, Node.js, Object-Oriented Programming, Cloud-native microservices
skill_match_scores: C#:0.0, TypeScript:1.0, Node.js:0.5, Object-Oriented Programming:1.0, Cloud-native microservices:1.0
must_have_quals: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related technical field, or equivalent practical experience, 5+ years of professional software engineering experience building production services or applications using OO languages such as C#, Java, or TypeScript/Node.js, 2+ years designing, building, and operating cloud-native microservices or APIs at scale (REST or gRPC), including version control, CI/CD pipelines, and automated testing as part of your daily workflow, Experience with relational and/or NoSQL data stores and data modeling for production services, Demonstrated ability to own features end-to-end, including design docs, implementation, testing strategy, rollout, and operational support
preferred_quals: Experience with Microsoft stack (C#, .NET, ASP.NET) in large-scale SaaS environments, Hands-on experience with Azure (e.g., AKS, App Services, Cosmos DB, Azure SQL) or comparable services in AWS, Prior work on shared platform components (libraries, pipelines, common services) adopted by multiple teams, Familiarity with observability and telemetry stacks used for monitoring and incident response (e.g., metrics, logging, dashboards, alerting, big data/stream processing for telemetry), Experience participating in or leading RCAs and on-call activities for high-severity incidents, with a focus on prevention and long-term reliability improvements, Experience working in an agile environment and delivering software iteratively with frequent, high-quality releases, Strong fundamentals in data structures, algorithms, distributed systems, and troubleshooting in a production environment, Clear, concise communication skills and the ability to work effectively in cross-functional and cross-team settings
core_responsibilities: Own feature delivery across the full software development lifecycle: design, implementation, testing, deployment, and operational support for backend services and APIs used by B2B products and platforms, Design and implement services and APIs using C# or TypeScript/Node.js, following best practices for modularity, testability, and maintainability, Build cloud-native, microservices-based systems, leveraging Azure services, containers, and modern deployment practices to achieve high availability, resilience, and scalability, Contribute to shared platform capabilities (e.g., libraries, config pipelines, telemetry and observability tooling) that are reused across multiple B2B services, helping other teams adopt and extend them, Improve quality and reliability by authoring and maintaining automated tests (unit, integration, contract, and end-to-end where appropriate), defining test plans for your features, and participating in on-call rotations with strong root cause analysis and incident response, Instrument services with telemetry (metrics, logs, traces) and use data to drive performance tuning, capacity planning, and error-budget–based decisions for the systems you own, Partner with product management, architecture, SRE, and peer application teams to refine requirements, align designs, and unblock cross-team initiatives, especially where platform capabilities or shared infrastructure are involved, Contribute to code reviews and engineering best practices, providing constructive, specific feedback and helping to evolve team standards around coding, testing, and deployment, Mentor more junior engineers informally by sharing context, reviewing designs, and providing guidance on debugging, testing, and operational excellence consistent with P3 expectations
job_summary: Docusign is a company that brings agreements to life, serving over 1.5 million customers globally. They are seeking a P3 Software Engineer to own the end-to-end delivery of critical infrastructure for their B2B agreement management workflows, focusing on designing, building, and operating scalable backend services and APIs.
